title: cornus alba var. occidentalis
intro: Cornus alba var. occidentalis, also known as the Western Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting cardiovascular health, due to its antioxidant and vasodilatory properties. Herbal preparations of Cornus alba var. occidentalis, such as infusions or decoctions, can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
